About: It is about an equestrian competition.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with a Croatian–Venetian victory in the Ottoman–Venetian war.
CreatedAround: It was created around the early 18th century.
CreatedTo: It was created to commemorate a victory.
HasA: It has a hanging metal ring.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by horsemen aiming lances at a hanging metal ring.
HeldIn: It was held in the Croatian town of Sinj.
HeldOn: It was held on August 14, 1715.
It was held on August 15.
It was held on the last day of the carnival.
It was held on May 9.
It was held on May 15.
It was held on July 6.
It was held on February 9.
It was held on April 19.
It was held on October 4.
It is held on Sunday of a first full weekend in August every year.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by knightly competitions.
IsA: It is a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.
KnownFor: It is known for commemorating a victory.
LocatedIn: It is located in the restored Kvartiri complex in Sinj.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Ottoman–Venetian war.
It occurred during Venetian rule in Dalmatia.
It occurred during the 300th anniversary celebrations of the Alka.
PartOf: It is part of the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists.
UsedAs: It is used as a target.
UsedFor: It is used for piercing with lances.
It is used for a competition.
It is used for awarding points.
UsedIn: It is used in the Sinjska alka.
It is used in the tournament.
UsedTo: It is used to hit with a spear.
It is used to award points.
